page: Amazon Web Services x8i.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors using the x86_64 architecture. The system features 128 virtual CPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 480 MB of L3 cache and 2048.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing 16.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Local storage is not bundled with this instance, and it relies on a Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. The hardware supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which contribute to top-tier performance in Passmark extended instructions and LLM inference prompt processing for both 7B and 70B models. While multi-threaded operations and cached memory bandwidth are in the top 10%, single-core CPU performance and Passmark memory scores fall into the average tier.

Given the high memory density of 16 GB per vCPU and strong multi-core capabilities, this server is designed for memory-intensive workloads. It is highly suitable for large-scale database operations, in-memory caching platforms like Redis, software compilation, and medium-to-large LLM inference tasks. The absence of local storage and dedicated GPUs represents a design tradeoff, positioning the instance as a cost-efficient choice for memory-bound and compute-heavy workloads that do not require local NVMe drives or hardware acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services x8i.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs on 64 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores running at 3.9 GHz. It is configured with 2048.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, offering a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. The server lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Powered by the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, high cached memory bandwidth, and strong multi-threaded decompression capabilities. Advanced instruction support, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX, enables top-tier performance in LLM inference prompt processing. This instance is optimized for memory-intensive applications, large database operations, Redis caching, and multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs, 2048 GB of DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors, designed for memory-intensive database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 128 vCPUs, 2048 GB DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 platform featuring two terabytes of DDR5 RAM and high-core-count Intel Xeon processors for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 64 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores running at 3.9 GHz
2048.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory providing 16.0 GB per vCPU
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and cached memory bandwidth
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated LLM prompt processing
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for in-memory databases, Redis, and large-scale enterprise applications
